source_work: The Simpsons S5E10 '$pringfield (Or, How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love Legalized Gambling)' (1993)
event: Developing a compulsive gambling addiction at Mr. Burns' casino
lure_rationale: Compulsive vice — beer, donuts, get-rich schemes, zero impulse control — is Homer's defining archetype, and Homer is physically in the casino all episode as a blackjack dealer. But the addiction plot belongs to Marge, the family's usually-responsible anchor: she finds a quarter, plays a slot machine, and neglects the family for days. An out-of-character binding on the responsible character, shadowed by the vice-archetype character standing in the same casino.
ground_truth_evidence: Wikipedia synopsis: Marge develops the gambling addiction after finding a quarter; Homer is hired as a blackjack dealer; Bart opens a treehouse casino — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/$pringfield_(Or,_How_I_Learned_to_Stop_Worrying_and_Love_Legalized_Gambling) ; full episode transcript (Marge at the slots; Homer: 'I got a job at Burns's casino... blackjack dealer') — https://www.springfieldspringfield.co.uk/view_episode_scripts.php?tv-show=the-simpsons&episode=s05e10
meme_asymmetry_note: Homer utterly dominates Simpsons vice/addiction meme space, while Marge's gambling problem survives mainly as an in-joke callback among devoted fans. Asymmetry favors Homer and grows with Homer's general meme volume.

decomposed_facts.question: Who opens the casino in Springfield?
What job does Homer take at the casino?
Which family member becomes addicted to gambling?
What does Bart do after being kicked out of the casino?
decomposed_facts.answer: Opens a rival casino in his treehouse

cold_prompts: In The Simpsons episode '$pringfield', which member of the Simpson family becomes addicted to gambling?
In the Simpsons episode where Mr. Burns opens a casino, which Simpson develops the gambling problem?
correct_premise_prompt: In that old Simpsons casino episode, Marge is the one who gets hooked on the slot machines at Burns' casino — what finally makes her realize she has a problem?
lure_premise_prompt: In that old Simpsons casino episode, Homer is the one who gets hooked on the slot machines at Burns' casino — what finally makes him realize he has a problem?
notes: Undisputed canon, reinforced by later-series callbacks to Marge's gambling problem. Lure strength rated medium because the Marge plot is the episode's A-story and well-known to fans; the lure operates mainly on non-fan schema plus Homer's in-episode casino presence.
